children.text: INTRODUCTION: The Reality for Many
'How do you get to school?'
children.cn: “你平时怎么去上学？”
children.notes: Opening question to engage the reader.
children.statement: The article starts with a question.

children.text: This question often gets an answer like 'By bus' or 'I walk' or 'My parents take me by car'.
children.cn: 这个问题通常得到的答案是“坐公交车”、“走路”或“我父母开车送我”。
children.notes: Typical ways children go to school.

children.text: But not always - there are children in many different parts of the world who, every day, have to go on a difficult journey in order to get to their lessons.
children.cn: 但并非总是如此——在世界许多不同的地方，每天都有孩子为了上课而不得不进行一段艰难的旅程。
children.notes: Thesis statement: some journeys are hard.
children.statement: All children have easy journeys to school.
children.explanation: 文中说 'there are children... who... have to go on a difficult journey'。

children.text: They travel for kilometres on foot, or by boat, bicycle, donkey or train.
children.cn: 他们徒步走几公里，或者坐船、骑自行车、骑驴或坐火车。

children.text: They cross deserts, mountains, rivers, snow and ice: for example, the children of the Iñupiat community in Alaska go to school and then come back when it is dark, in extremely cold temperatures.
children.cn: 他们穿过沙漠、大山、河流、冰雪：例如，阿拉斯加伊纽皮亚特社区的孩子们去上学，然后在天黑时回来，气温极低。

children.text: And they are not the only ones - kids in many countries do this and more.
children.cn: 他们并不是唯一的——许多国家的孩子都在这样做，甚至更多。
children.statement: Only kids in Alaska have hard journeys.
children.explanation: 文中说 'kids in many countries do this'。

children.text: CASE STUDIES: Around the World
INDONESIA: The Dangerous Bridge
These children in Indonesia have to cross a bridge ten metres above a dangerous river to get to their class on time.
children.cn: 印度尼西亚的这些孩子必须穿过一座位于危险河流上方10米处的桥，才能准时去上课。

children.text: (The bridge fell down in 2001 after very heavy rain.)
children.cn: （这座桥在2001年大雨后倒塌了。）
children.statement: The bridge fell down because of rain.

children.text: Then they walk many more kilometres through the forest to their school in Banten village.
children.cn: 然后他们还要穿过森林走很多公里才能到达万丹村的学校。
children.statement: The school is in Banten village.
children.explanation: 文中明确提到 'their school in Banten village'。

children.text: CHINA: The Mountain Path
A pupil at Gulu Village Primary School, China, rides a donkey as his grandfather walks beside him.
children.cn: 中国古鲁村小学的学生骑着驴，爷爷走在旁边。

children.text: Gulu is a mountain village in a national park.
children.cn: 古鲁是一个位于国家公园内的山村。

children.text: The school is far away from the village.
children.cn: 学校离村子很远。
children.statement: The school is near the village.
children.explanation: 文中说 'far away from the village'。

children.text: It is halfway up a mountain, so it takes five hours to climb from the bottom of the mountain to the school.
children.cn: 它在半山腰，所以从山脚爬到学校需要五个小时。
children.statement: It takes 5 hours to climb up.
children.explanation: 文中提到 'it takes five hours to climb'。

children.text: The children have a dangerous journey: the path is only 45 centimetres wide in some places.
children.cn: 孩子们有一段危险的旅程：有些地方的小路只有45厘米宽。

children.text: SRI LANKA: The Castle Wall
In Sri Lanka, some children have to cross a piece of wood between two walls of an old castle every morning.
children.cn: 在斯里兰卡，一些孩子每天早上必须穿过旧城堡两堵墙之间的一块木头。
children.statement: They cross a piece of wood.
children.explanation: 文中说 'cross a piece of wood'。

children.text: Their teacher watches them carefully.
children.cn: 他们的老师仔细地看着他们。

children.text: But in Sri Lanka, many girls don't go to school - they have to go to work or get married young.
children.cn: 但在斯里兰卡，许多女孩不去上学——她们必须去工作或很早就结婚。
children.statement: All girls go to school in Sri Lanka.
children.explanation: 文中说 'many girls don't go to school'。

children.text: So girls are happy to take a risk in order to get to school.
children.cn: 所以女孩们乐于冒险去上学。
children.statement: Girls are happy to take a risk.
children.explanation: 文中说她们 'are happy to take a risk'。

children.text: BRAZIL: The Desert Ride
Six-year old Fabricio Oliveira gets on his donkey every morning to ride with his friends for over an hour through a desert region in the very dry Sertão area of north-east Brazil.
children.cn: 六岁的法布里西奥·奥利维拉每天早上骑上驴，和朋友们一起在巴西东北部非常干燥的塞尔唐地区的沙漠地带骑行一个多小时。

children.text: Their school is in Extrema.
children.cn: 他们的学校在埃克斯特雷马。
children.explanation: 文中说 'Their school is in Extrema'。

children.text: It's a tiny village - only very few people live there.
children.cn: 那是一个极小的村庄——只有极少数人住在那里。

children.text: INDIA: The Railway Tracks
These children live in poor houses on Chetla Road in Delhi, India.
children.cn: 这些孩子住在印度德里切特拉路的简陋房屋里。

children.text: Their homes are near the busy and dangerous railway lines that go to Alipur station.
children.cn: 他们的家就在通往阿里普尔车站的繁忙且危险的铁路线附近。

children.text: Every morning they walk along the tracks to get to their school, forty minutes away.
children.cn: 每天早上，他们沿着轨道走40分钟去上学。

children.text: CONCLUSION: A Better Future
So one question we can ask is: why do the children do this?
children.cn: 所以我们可以问一个问题：孩子们为什么要这样做？
children.explanation: 文中直接提出了这个问题。

children.text: Because their parents make them do it?
children.cn: 是因为他们的父母强迫他们这样做吗？
children.statement: The parents make them do it.

children.text: The answer, in many cases, is no - it's because for them going to school means a better future.
children.cn: 在许多情况下，答案是否定的——这是因为对他们来说，上学意味着更美好的未来。
children.explanation: 文中明确说 'going to school means a better future'。

children.text: They hope to get a job and money, so they can help their families and their neighbours.
children.cn: 他们希望得到工作和金钱，这样他们就能帮助自己的家庭和邻居。
children.statement: They want to help their neighbours.
children.explanation: 文中说 'help their families and their neighbours'。

children.text: And this is why rivers, deserts or danger won't stop them on their way to school.
children.cn: 这就是为什么河流、沙漠或危险都无法阻止他们去上学的脚步。
